{"step_by_step_thinking": "The documents provide information about the sickle cell mutation, which is a substitution of a single nucleotide in the beta-globin gene. This substitution results in the replacement of glutamic acid with valine. Such a change in a single base pair is characteristic of a point mutation. None of the other options (selective mutation, frame shift mutation, or nonsense mutation) describe a single base pair change.", "answer_choice": "A"}
